releases.headline: Encrypted Backup, Bot Guard & Security Hardening
releases.summary: Adds automated S3-compatible backup with a passphrase-encrypted vault key and Cloudflare R2 as a supported provider. Bundles in the v1.4.3 through v1.4.8 hardening run: sync enrollment codes, POST-only logout with CSRF, WebSocket Authorization-header-only auth, public-GET bot guard, admin-configurable /health CORS, locally vendored Google Fonts with tightened CSP, and an ECIES protocol version byte for algorithm agility.

items.title: Automated encrypted backup to S3-compatible storage
items.body: Scheduled backups encrypt the vault key with an operator passphrase before upload. Restore requires the same passphrase, so a stolen bucket cannot be used to recover data.

items.title: Cloudflare R2 as a storage provider
items.body: R2 is supported alongside generic S3 endpoints for both file storage and the new backup destination. Configure via the existing S3 settings using the R2 endpoint URL.

items.title: Sync enrollment codes
items.body: A one-time enrollment code replaces manual cert-file transfer when pairing a sync client. The code is single-use, short-lived, and bound to the issuing account.

items.title: Bot guard middleware on public GET routes
items.body: Request fingerprinting on accept-language, sec-fetch-dest, and sec-fetch-mode blocks automated clients from public GET endpoints. Real browsers pass through unchanged.

items.title: ECIES protocol version byte
items.body: The `_ek` field is now prefixed with a one-byte protocol version so future KEM or cipher changes can be negotiated without breaking older sessions. Current version is 0x01 (ML-KEM-1024 + P-384 + HKDF-SHA3-512 + XChaCha20-Poly1305).

items.title: Logo and OG image variants
items.body: Adds image assets used by the landing page and social-card metadata. No behavior change for existing deployments.

items.title: Logout is POST-only with CSRF validation
items.body: GET-based logout is removed. Clients must POST to the logout endpoint with a valid CSRF token, closing a cross-site logout-forgery vector.

items.title: WebSocket authentication via Authorization header only
items.body: Query-string API key tokens are no longer accepted on WebSocket upgrades. Clients must send the key in the Authorization header, keeping it out of access logs and proxy URLs.

items.title: `/health` CORS origins are admin-configurable
items.body: Operators running a PQC gateway in front of the server can now allowlist specific origins for `/health` checks via the admin UI, replacing the previous static configuration.

items.title: Google Fonts vendored locally with tightened CSP
items.body: Fonts ship as woff2 files served from the same origin, removing the external CDN dependency. The Content-Security-Policy drops the corresponding font-src exception.

items.title: Landing page refreshed for Customer Stash, sync, and enrollment
items.body: Copy and screenshots reflect the current feature set including Customer Stash, the sync client, and one-time enrollment codes.
